portfolio: Infrastructure, Transport, Regional Development, Communications, Sport & Arts
executive_summary: CASA is an independent statutory (corporate Commonwealth) entity and aviation safety regulator funded by an aviation fuel excise, government appropriation, and regulatory fees/levies. The plan is dominated by two pressures: a structurally challenged funding model (budgeted operating deficits of $14.7m in 2025-26 widening to ~$20m, drawing down reserves, with fuel-excise revenue threatened by fleet electrification and fuel-efficiency shifts), and rapid industry change (RPAS/drones now exceed flight-crew licences, advanced air mobility/eVTOL certification, Western Sydney Airport opening 2026, and the new Australian Future Airspace Framework). CASA is mid-stream on a multi-year flight-operations and airworthiness regulatory transition, implementing a National Oversight Plan to move to risk-based surveillance (responding to ANAO recommendations), expanding service-delivery transparency dashboards, and modernising core systems (Enterprise Aviation Processing, myCASA, Medical Records System) under a Digital Business Strategy with Essential Eight cyber uplift. As a small, expertise-heavy regulator with admitted financial-sustainability, workforce and digital pressures, the best-fit demand sits in Financial & Accounting, Digital & ICT Delivery, Cybersecurity, Data/Analytics and assurance/governance — not large delivery programs.

strategic_priorities.title: Flight operations & airworthiness regulatory transition
strategic_priorities.description: Continue the multi-year transition to the flight operations regulations (introduced 2021) and reform of airworthiness rules, including new CASR Part 43, Part 92 dangerous goods, Part 66 engineer licensing, and Parts 103/131. CASA explicitly states the legislative and industry transition will continue for several years.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: The legislative and industry transition is expected to continue for several years.

strategic_priorities.title: Integrating emerging aviation technologies
strategic_priorities.description: Support safe integration of RPAS/drones, advanced air mobility (eVTOL, e.g. Joby JAS4-1 type certification), uncrewed traffic management (UTM), vertiports, and consult on the Australian Future Airspace Framework (AFAF). Drone licence holders now exceed flight-crew licences.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: During 2025, CASA will consult on the development of the Australian Future Airspace Framework (AFAF).

strategic_priorities.title: Service-delivery transparency and timeliness
strategic_priorities.description: Expand the online service-delivery dashboard, deliver against the September 2024 service charter, establish published timeframes for 75% of regulatory services in 2025-26 (100% by 2026-27) and meet timeframes 80% of the time. CASA admits the timeframe-development target has not been achieved since 2019-20.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: Planned outcome for development of regulatory service timeframes have not been achieved since introduced in 2019-20.

strategic_priorities.title: National Oversight Plan / risk-based surveillance
strategic_priorities.description: Implement the National Oversight Plan to consolidate oversight into a risk-based surveillance program, explicitly aligned to ANAO recommendations and ICAO critical elements, with improved data capture and systems-level trend analysis.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: This allows us to move towards a risk-based surveillance program in line with recommendations by the Australian National Audit Office.

strategic_priorities.title: Digital modernisation and cyber uplift
strategic_priorities.description: Continue the Digital Business Strategy 2023-2026: invest $7.3m capital in 2025-26 (and further in forward years) in the Enterprise Aviation Processing (EAP) system, myCASA portal, Medical Records System and network operations, while lifting cyber maturity to Essential Eight and modernising gateway security.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: investments centre on enhanced functionality of CASA's Enterprise Aviation Processing (EAP) system, myCASA portal...

strategic_priorities.title: Workforce capability and culture
strategic_priorities.description: Implement the Strategic Workforce Plan 2024-2027 and People and Culture Strategy 2022-2027 to attract/retain a dispersed, highly professional workforce amid industry-wide skills shortages (engineers, pilots) and build skills for emerging-technology regulation.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: The implementation of our Strategic Workforce Plan 2024-2027 is designed to support CASA being equipped with a high-performing workforce.

reform_and_change_drivers: Aviation White Paper and revised whole-of-government Regulatory Policy, Practice and Performance Framework giving CASA responsibility for safe rollout of new technologies and reducing GA regulatory burden
Ministerial Statement of Expectations (2023-25) driving surveillance, transparency, service-delivery and regulatory-program obligations
Rapid sector growth: 46% increase in authorisation holders and ~50% increase in pilot licences over five years; RPAS licence holders now exceed flight-crew licences
Major infrastructure changes: Western Sydney International Airport opening 2026, new runways at Perth and Melbourne
Emerging technology: advanced air mobility/eVTOL certification, UTM Action Plan, vertiports, MOSAIC airworthiness rule-making, space-launch activity
Funding-model fragility: fuel-excise revenue (3.556 cents/litre) threatened by fuel-efficiency gains and electrification; RPAS/aerodrome/international-carrier oversight does not contribute to excise
ANAO recommendations driving the move to risk-based surveillance via the National Oversight Plan
ICAO obligations including USOAP Continuous Monitoring Approach
challenges_and_risks: Budgeted operating deficits every year of the plan ($14.7m, $20.5m, $16.7m, $16.7m), funded by drawing down retained surpluses, cash and investment reserves — an explicitly unsustainable trajectory
Strategic risk that CASA 'fails to maintain positive financial stability' given uncertainty over the medium-to-long-term funding model
Cybersecurity strategic risk (appetite 'Small') with an active program to reach Essential Eight and modernise gateway security
Workforce/skills shortages (licensed engineers, pilots) affecting both industry and CASA's own capability to regulate emerging technologies
Persistent failure to establish regulatory service-delivery timeframes (target unmet since 2019-20) despite it now being a headline transparency commitment
Many performance targets are newly introduced or 'same as 2025-26' with qualitative 'trend' measures, and some past results 'could not be confirmed due to issues with data currency'
Ageing/under-development core systems (EAP, myCASA, Medical Records System) requiring sustained capital while budgets are in deficit
Strategic risk that CASA is 'unable to adapt to the evolving aviation environment' given the pace of RPAS/AAM change

opportunity_areas.agency_need: CASA budgets operating deficits across all four years, is drawing down reserves, and faces erosion of its fuel-excise revenue base as fleets become more fuel-efficient and electrify — while RPAS/aerodrome/international oversight generates no excise.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A financial-sustainability review and cost-to-serve/activity-based costing analysis of regulatory services, modelling alternative fee/levy and funding-model options to inform advice to the Board and the Department/Minister.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan names financial stability as a strategic risk and explicitly states excise revenue is under pressure and that the funding model's sustainability is being engaged on with stakeholders — a clean, evidenced fit for costing and financial-sustainability advice.

opportunity_areas.area: Core systems modernisation (EAP, myCASA, Medical Records System)
opportunity_areas.agency_need: CASA is investing $7.3m in 2025-26 and further in forward years to enhance the Enterprise Aviation Processing system, myCASA portal, Medical Records System and network operations, and wants to shift more transactions to digital channels.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Discovery and service design plus build/integration support for EAP and myCASA enhancements, including business/process analysis to streamline manual application processing and a UX uplift to lift digital-channel uptake.
opportunity_areas.rationale: Named, budgeted multi-year capital investment in specific systems, paired with an explicit strategy to 'make it quicker and easier to interact digitally' and a new digital-submission performance measure — the firm can build and implement, not just advise.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: CASA identifies cyber as a strategic risk (appetite 'Small') and is running an approved program of work to improve cyber maturity to Essential Eight, meet the PSPF, and modernise network gateway security.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: An Essential Eight maturity assessment and PSPF-alignment review with a prioritised remediation roadmap, plus security assessment/assurance of the modernised gateway and core systems (EAP, MRS).
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan names a specific, funded cyber program tied to a strategic risk and concrete frameworks (Essential Eight, PSPF) and gateway modernisation — a direct, time-bound fit.

opportunity_areas.area: Data and analytics for the National Oversight Plan
opportunity_areas.agency_need: CASA is implementing a risk-based surveillance program (NOP) that depends on combining surveillance data with other safety data sources for systems-level trend analysis, and admits to past 'data currency' problems affecting performance reporting.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A safety-data analytics and information-management uplift: data-source integration, business intelligence/dashboards for risk-based surveillance prioritisation, and an information-management/data-quality remediation to fix currency issues.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The NOP explicitly relies on data analysis combined across sources for risk-based decisions, and the plan concedes prior data-currency issues — a credible analytics and information-management opportunity supporting an ANAO-driven reform.

opportunity_areas.area: Service-delivery timeframe framework and performance measurement
opportunity_areas.agency_need: CASA must publish timeframes for 75% of regulatory services in 2025-26 and meet them 80% of the time, but admits this target has not been achieved since 2019-20; several performance measures are newly introduced or vague 'trend' measures.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A service-standards and PGPA-aligned performance-measure design: establish defined service-delivery timeframes across regulatory services, an underpinning measurement methodology, and strengthened performance measures for the corporate plan/annual performance statements.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan openly flags a long-standing unmet target and a strong transparency/service-charter agenda, with multiple soft 'trend' measures — a clear performance-framework and service-design need.

opportunity_areas.area: Delivery support for the flight-operations / airworthiness regulatory transition
opportunity_areas.agency_need: A multi-year regulatory transition (flight operations, Parts 43/66/92/103/131) plus the RPAS/AAM Roadmap, GA Workplan and AFAF must be delivered alongside growing business-as-usual workloads.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Program/PMO support and change-management for the regulatory transition portfolio — delivery scheduling, benefits/transition tracking against the Forward Regulatory Program, and industry-transition change support.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan names multiple concurrent multi-year reform programs and stresses delivery must be balanced against BAU growth; however CASA is a small regulator that runs these largely in-house, so demand is for targeted delivery/PMO support rather than large embedded programs.

opportunity_areas.area: Post-implementation review / evaluation (Class 5 medical scheme, flight ops rules)
opportunity_areas.agency_need: CASA commits to a post-implementation review of its Class 5 medical scheme and to 'realising intended safety improvements' from the 2021 flight operations rules.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: An independent post-implementation review/evaluation of the Class 5 medical scheme (and optionally the flight-operations reforms), with an evaluation framework, stakeholder analysis and recommendations.
opportunity_areas.rationale: Named, scheduled review with a clear evaluation scope — a proportionate short-to-medium advisory engagement well suited to a small regulator.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: CASA is implementing a Strategic Workforce Plan 2024-2027 amid industry-wide skills shortages and a need to build new skills (cyber, AI, software assurance, eVTOL/RPAS) to regulate emerging technologies.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A targeted workforce capability assessment and planning piece focused on emerging-technology skills (certification, software assurance, cyber, digitalisation), with attraction/retention strategies for hard-to-fill specialist roles.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan names the workforce plan and admits skills shortages and a need to understand future capability requirements; demand is real but general rather than tied to a single named initiative.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: CASA has refreshed its 7 strategic risks (June 2025) and relies on its Board, BARC 'deep dives' and internal audit for assurance, alongside an enterprise quality and risk management framework.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Independent assurance/health checks of high-priority strategic-risk controls (e.g. cyber, financial sustainability, NOP implementation) and quality-management/internal-control review support to the Board Audit and Risk Committee.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan details an active risk-oversight model with BARC deep dives and internal audit, providing scope for independent assurance; the need is inferred from the governance structure rather than a named procurement.

recommended_actions.action: Approach the CFO / Chief Finance function with a financial-sustainability and cost-to-serve proposition framed around the funding-model risk and eroding excise base.
recommended_actions.rationale: The plan's standout, repeatedly stated pressure is structural deficits and a fragile excise model; a costing/sustainability review is the most defensible, board-relevant entry point and is comparatively apolitical 'enabling' work.

recommended_actions.action: Prepare a capability statement and meeting request to the CIO / Digital Business Strategy owner covering EAP/myCASA build support, business-process analysis and UX, plus an Essential Eight maturity assessment.
recommended_actions.rationale: Named, budgeted multi-year digital and cyber investments with explicit targets (digital-submission uptake, Essential Eight) create concrete, fundable demand the firm can both advise on and build.

recommended_actions.action: Offer a short briefing to the Safety Intelligence/oversight function on data integration and BI for the National Oversight Plan, referencing the ANAO-driven move to risk-based surveillance and the plan's admitted data-currency issues.
recommended_actions.rationale: The NOP is an active reform with a clear data dependency and acknowledged data-quality weaknesses, but engagement requires understanding CASA's existing safety-data systems first.

recommended_actions.action: Pitch a performance-measure and service-standards design engagement to the Strategic Business Planning / service-delivery owner, anchored on the long-unmet timeframe target.
recommended_actions.rationale: CASA has publicly committed to publishing service timeframes and concedes the target has been missed since 2019-20 — a visible problem with a near-term deadline (75% in 2025-26).

recommended_actions.action: Position for the Class 5 medical scheme post-implementation review as a discrete, proportionate evaluation engagement.
recommended_actions.rationale: It is a named, scheduled review with clear scope, ideal for a first low-risk engagement that can build a relationship for larger work.

recommended_actions.action: Monitor CASA procurement (AusTender) and the Forward Regulatory Program for go-to-market on EAP/myCASA enhancements, cyber uplift and surveillance-systems work.
recommended_actions.rationale: Capital investments are flagged for 2025-26 to 2028-29; tracking the actual approaches to market lets the firm time bids and pre-position.

engagement_targets: Chief Finance Officer / Finance function (funding-model and costing work)
Chief Information Officer / owner of the Digital Business Strategy 2023-2026 (EAP, myCASA, MRS, cyber)
Head of the surveillance/oversight function and the Safety Intelligence and Analysis team (National Oversight Plan, data analytics)
Section Manager, Strategic Business Planning (corporate plan, performance measures, service standards — named contact in the plan)
Chief Risk Officer and Board Audit and Risk Committee (BARC) secretariat (assurance and risk-framework work)
People & Culture function and the Board People and Culture Sub-Committee (workforce planning)
Regulatory development / Forward Regulatory Program leads (transition program and Class 5 medical review)
talking_points: Your plan budgets deficits every year to 2028-29 and flags excise erosion from electrification — we can build a cost-to-serve/ABC model and test alternative funding options to support the Board's case to the Department and Minister.

You're investing in EAP, myCASA and the Medical Records System while pushing more transactions to digital channels — we can run the discovery, redesign the processes and build/integrate the enhancements, not just advise.
Reaching Essential Eight and modernising your gateway is a named cyber program against a 'Small' risk appetite — we can assess maturity, map PSPF gaps and assure the rebuilt systems.

Your National Oversight Plan depends on combining safety data for risk-based surveillance, and you've acknowledged past data-currency issues — we can integrate the data and stand up the BI that makes risk-based prioritisation real.

You've committed to publishing regulatory service timeframes but the target has been unmet since 2019-20 — we can design the service standards and the underlying performance measures to close that gap before the 2025-26 deadline.

The Class 5 medical scheme post-implementation review and the flight-operations transition are discrete, evaluable pieces of work where independent evaluation can demonstrate the safety benefits you've promised.
caveats: This analysis is based solely on the corporate plan; the external research returned no usable results (the web-search tool hit a rate limit), so news, ANAO and parliamentary sections are empty — notably, the plan itself references ANAO recommendations behind the National Oversight Plan, which a future search should verify and could strengthen the assurance/data opportunities. CASA is a small, expertise-heavy regulator that delivers most regulatory and reform work in-house, so program-delivery demand is more limited than for a large service-delivery agency; the strongest, most defensible opportunities are the enabling/corporate ones (financial sustainability, digital, cyber, data). Several opportunities are inferred from structural signals (governance, workforce) rather than named procurements and are marked Medium accordingly. No individuals are named beyond the Chair, CEO/Director and the Strategic Business Planning section manager listed in the plan.
